name: RunsOn
description: Self-hosted GitHub Actions runners that live in your own AWS account on EC2 (spot or on-demand). No per-minute markup — you pay raw EC2 cost. Claims ~90% cheaper than GH-hosted. The RunsOn control plane is a CloudFormation stack you deploy; this integration wraps its admin REST API for inspecting runners, jobs, and spend.

credential_fields.name: stack_url
credential_fields.label: Stack URL
credential_fields.description: The hostname your RunsOn CloudFormation stack exposes (e.g. runs-on.acme.com)

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Admin API Key
credential_fields.description: Admin API key set via the ADMIN_API_KEY parameter when deploying the RunsOn stack

tools.name: list_runners
tools.description: List active EC2 runner instances managed by this RunsOn stack.

tools.name: list_jobs
tools.description: List recent GitHub Actions jobs the stack picked up. Args: status? (queued|running|completed), limit?.

tools.name: get_job
tools.description: Get details + logs URL for a single job. Args: job_id.

tools.name: terminate_runner
tools.description: Force-terminate a runner instance (the EC2 instance will be stopped). Args: instance_id.

tools.name: get_spend
tools.description: Get raw EC2 spend attributed to RunsOn (read from Cost Explorer). Args: period? (today|7d|30d|month).
